title: Youth Residential Support Worker
description:
Are you looking for a flexible role where you can make a real impact on the lives of young people in care?
Purpose of the Job
As a Children's mainstream Residential Support Worker, you'll provide essential support to young people aged 12-17 living in mainstream residential homes. Your role is to offer stability, promote independence, and create a safe, nurturing environment where young people can thrive.
This is
ad-hoc, temporary work,
ideal for those seeking flexibility while still wanting to make a meaningful difference.
Key Details of the Job
Location:
various locations in Leeds
Pay:
£14.82 per hour + uplifts for waking nights and weekends
Contract:
Ad-hoc temporary work (no guaranteed hours)
Shifts:
Earlies, lates, sleep-ins, waking nights
Requirements
Ligature Training (mandatory)
Minimum 1 year experience
in residential childcare, youth support, safeguarding, or a similar environment
Strong understanding of safeguarding, risk management, and de-escalation
Experience engaging positively with young people who may display challenging behaviours
Desirable
Experience in Mental Health Secure settings
Additional behavioural management or risk-reduction training
Apply Today
If you're ready to support young people and work flexibly around your lifestyle, we'd love to hear from you.
summary:
Flexible temporary residential support role for young people in care across Leeds.
Includes safeguarding-focused support, behavioural management and varied shift patterns.

title: Behaviour Support Worker
company: Veritas Education Recruitment Ltd • Agency • Contract
description:
Job: Behaviour Support Worker
Type: Long Term Contract
Start Date: April 2026
Full Time - Leeds
Veritas Education are working with an alternative provision within the Leeds area who are looking for an experienced Behaviour Support Worker, to start from after the Easter holidays in April 2026 until the end of the academic year.
The provision supports children from 11-16 years old, who are currently on temporary exclusion, or facing a fixed permanent exclusion from mainstream schooling. The provision is set up with fantastic facilities, which enables students to develop academically, socially and emotionally, and helps facilitate their transition back into either mainstream schools or specialist provisions.
The role of a behaviour support worker will be to support key children on a 1:1 basis across their learning, working with them during their lessons, help them learn self-regulation and emotional control, but also manage behaviour and expectations across the setting.
The ideal staff member will be someone who is experienced in supporting SEMH/Behavioural needs, confident working with children on a one-to-one basis, and pro-active when it comes to getting involved within the student's core and vocational lessons to help create an engaging and inclusive learning environment!
Veritas Education can also offer essential training for this role such as Level 2 Team Teach training, to ensure you know the required behaviour management and de-escalation skills to support learners in crisis.
Please contact George Walton at
summary:
Long-term Leeds contract supporting pupils with behavioural and SEMH needs in an alternative provision.
Focuses on 1:1 classroom support, regulation and behaviour management.

title: Support Worker
description:
Are you good with people? Would you like to use your skills to support individuals to get into meaningful employment? We have a unique role working as a Support Worker in our Courtyard Apartments.
At Courtyard Services Horsforth, Leeds you will support people with learning disabilities, encouraging and empowering them to develop skills and confidence in a warm and vibrant setting.
The Role:
You will be supporting people in their own homes and in the community to live independently and to make choices and decisions about their lives. The successful candidate will also support our tenants to gain life skills and confidence to become more independent and move into sustained employment.
This support worker role in Horsforth, Leeds includes:
Providing support in daily living activities, including practical assistance where skills are not yet developed.
Providing support with household tasks and domestic duties, such as cooking, cleaning, laundry and maintaining the environment to a high standard.
Accompanying clients to appointments, social outings and activities.
Offering emotional support and promoting positive wellbeing.
Ensuring accurate records are maintained and safeguarding procedures are followed.
The ideal candidate:
Will have previous experience as a Support Worker, Care Assistant, Key Worker or in a similar role.
Will be passionate about helping others to develop independence and confidence.
Will be patient, reliable and able to build positive relationships.
Will be comfortable supporting people with learning disabilities.
Benefits:
£12.85 per hour
Location: Horsforth, Leeds
Opportunity to make a real difference in people's lives
Supportive team environment
Training and development opportunities
summary:
Leeds support worker role helping adults with learning disabilities build independence and move toward employment.
Includes home-based support, daily living assistance and community access.

title: SEN Support Workers
description:
SEN Support Workers Needed - Halifax & Huddersfield - Immediate Starts & September Roles!
Are you passionate about supporting children with additional needs? Do you have experience with SEN - whether through work, education, or personal/family life? If so, we want to hear from you!
At , we are urgently recruiting SEN Support Workers for a range of roles across Halifax and Huddersfield in specialist schools, alternative provisions, and SEN units. Opportunities are available for immediate start as well as from September 2025, on both a full-time and part-time basis.
The Role:
As an SEN Support Worker, you will:
Support pupils with a range of additional needs including autism, ADHD, SEMH, and complex learning difficulties
Work 1:1 or with small groups to deliver tailored support and encouragement
Help students engage with learning, social development, and emotional regulation
Support classroom routines and behaviour in a calm, consistent manner
Collaborate with teaching staff, therapists, and families where needed
The Ideal Candidate Will Have:
Experience working with children or young people with special educational needs (in school, care, youth work, or similar settings)
A patient, resilient, and compassionate approach
Strong communication and relationship-building skills
A genuine passion for helping young people succeed
Enhanced DBS on the update service (or willingness to apply)
Why Work With ?
Competitive daily pay
Local roles to suit your preferences and availability
Ongoing support from a dedicated education consultant
Free accredited training courses available
A chance to make a real difference in pupils' lives every day
summary:
SEN support opportunities across Halifax and Huddersfield in schools, SEN units and alternative provisions.
Covers 1:1 and small-group support for pupils with autism, ADHD, SEMH and complex needs.

title: SEMH Support Worker
description:
SEMH Support Worker - Wakefield (Secondary & Specialist Settings)
Position:
SEMH Teaching Assistant / Behaviour Support
Location:
Wakefield
Contract:
Full-time, long-term
Salary:
£475 - £550 per week (DOE)
About the Role
Join a specialist SEMH provision dedicated to supporting young people who face emotional, behavioural and mental health barriers to learning. Each day brings the opportunity to rebuild trust, encourage resilience, and help students re-engage with education in a meaningful way.
As an SEMH Support Worker, you will work closely with students who may present with challenging behaviour, trauma backgrounds, attachment needs, and complex emotional profiles. You will play a crucial role in providing consistency, emotional regulation support, and positive role modelling throughout the school day.
Key Responsibilities
Build strong, professional relationships with students to support behaviour, engagement, and wellbeing
Provide in-class and pastoral support on a 1:1 and small-group basis
Promote emotional regulation, coping strategies, and restorative approaches
Support students with transitions, routines, and de-escalation when needed
Collaborate with teachers, SENCOs, pastoral teams, and external professionals
Essential Requirements
Previous experience working with young people with SEMH, behavioural, or trauma-related needs (school, care, youth work, or similar)
A calm, patient, and consistent approach in challenging situations
Strong communication and behaviour management skills
A valid DBS on the update service (or willingness to apply)
summary:
Wakefield SEMH support role in specialist settings working with students facing behavioural and emotional barriers.
Blends in-class, pastoral and de-escalation support on a long-term basis.

title: Relief Support Worker
description:
We are seeking warm, motivated, and person-centred Relief Support Workers to join our team in Wakefield. You will be providing personalised care and support to adults with learning disabilities, each with unique support needs. This role requires promoting active and independent lifestyles in line with the principles of Creative Support.
In this role, you will offer respectful personal care, practical and emotional support, and assist with home management and social activities. This includes helping service users engage in community opportunities such as volunteering, education, and leisure activities.
Working flexibly across various shifts including evenings, weekends, and public holidays, you will support service users in all aspects of daily life and personal development. You will collaborate with colleagues, families, and professionals to ensure a high standard of care and maintain clear, accurate records.
This is an excellent opportunity for those pursuing a career in social care and seeking to develop specialist skills in a rewarding and varied environment.
Benefits of working with Creative Support include:
A one-off bonus of £100 after successful completion of the 4-month probationary period
20 days Annual Leave plus 8 days statutory bank holidays (pro rata for part-time employees)
Pension with company contribution
Company paid enhanced DBS for all staff
Free employee support programme
Free and subsidised mandatory training including the Care Certificate and Social Care Diploma
summary:
Wakefield relief role supporting adults with learning disabilities through personal care, daily living and community activity support.
Flexible shifts with training, DBS and probation bonus included.

title: Crisis Support Worker
description:
Do you have the commitment and positive values to
make a difference
to the lives of people living in
Bradford
?
As a Crisis Support Worker you will be providing person centred recovery focused support to adults with mental health needs accessing our crisis service in Shipley, Bradford. You will work with service users to develop a recovery support plan based on their presenting needs, wishes and circumstances. This will include offering support around managing mental wellbeing, mood, emotions, personal care, household tasks, domestic duties, accessing services in the local community and social and leisure opportunities.
Vacancy Reference Number: 83516
Applications for this role must be submitted via the Creative Support website using the above vacancy reference number.
You will be paid on a weekly basis.
This is a relief position which enables you to pick up shifts at a pace that suits you.
We are looking for warm, flexible and motivated individuals to provide person centred support to people with mental health support needs in crisis. You will need to be able to work flexible shifts and to be willing to work as part of a team in a dynamic and varied service.
Experience is essential for all our Relief roles.
Benefits of working with Creative Support:
A one-off bonus of £100 upon successful completion of the 4-month probationary period
20 days Annual Leave plus 8 days statutory bank holiday
Pension with company contribution
Company paid enhanced DBS for all staff
All our staff are supported 24/7 by our out-of-hours teams
Support to complete the nationally recognised Care Certificate and Social Care Diploma
summary:
Bradford crisis support role working with adults with mental health needs in a recovery-focused service.
Relief shifts cover wellbeing, daily living and community access support.
